evidence: In `src/domains/generation/types/generationParams.ts`, every declared field in `GenerationParams` is optional (`prompt?`, `seed?`, etc.), so the type does not express any required invariant for valid generation input.
The same interface includes an unrestricted index signature (`[key: string]: unknown`), which permits arbitrary misspelled or unsupported keys to pass type-checking.
In `src/domains/generation/types/index.ts`, `GenerationParams` is re-exported from the domain barrel, spreading this loose error contract across many consumers.
`GenerationParams` mixes persistence-style snake_case keys (`base_prompt`, `guidance_scale`, `num_inference_steps`) directly in a domain-shared type.
The barrel in `src/domains/generation/types/index.ts` re-exports this boundary-shaped type as a first-class domain contract, rather than isolating translation at a seam.
The broad index signature in `GenerationParams` adds ad-hoc extension behavior at the same boundary, increasing glue-code ambiguity for callers.

findings.summary: GenerationParams is too permissive, letting invalid inputs bypass typed failure paths.
evidence: All known fields are optional in `GenerationParams`, so missing critical values are type-valid.
The `[key: string]: unknown` index signature allows arbitrary keys and typoed params without compile-time feedback.
The barrel export makes this permissive contract the shared default across generation call paths.
findings.suggestion: Split strict core fields from extensibility: define required/validated core keys, replace the global index signature with an explicit `extra?: Record<string, unknown>`, and validate unknown payloads at ingestion before exposing `GenerationParams` to the rest of the domain.

findings.summary: Storage-oriented param naming is exported as the domain API without an explicit translation seam.
evidence: `GenerationParams` encodes snake_case keys that appear persistence/API-oriented rather than internal TS domain conventions.
`index.ts` re-exports `GenerationParams` directly, so callers consume boundary naming instead of a normalized domain shape.
No separate mapped type in this module distinguishes persistence contract from internal orchestration contract.
findings.suggestion: Introduce two explicit contracts (e.g., `PersistedGenerationParams` and `GenerationParams`), keep snake_case only at storage/API edges, and add a single mapper at the boundary so internal handoffs stay consistent and predictable.
